Process to buy talked through, real pain points and strong leaning to Zycus. CTO (John) recommended they speak to us) Next- we will do gap analysis and help Sherald build business case- recommendation to EXCO in late Jan. 19th Nov Overview meeting- will now follow up for F2F meeting & demo- more work needs to be done with their mgmt for business case Prospect to share their current system landscape, integrations, and process flow To set-up F2F demo in Gamuda's KL office
hard.ais_why:
• Across 1,026 chars of NextStep covering eight dated entries from Nov 2025 to Mar 2026 ‚Äî including champion buy-in (Sherald Siew Mei Yap, Snr Mgr; Joey Lye Phooi Wah, Asst GM Contracts), CTO recommendation, fit-gap analysis, business case to EXCO ‚Äî zero mention of AI, Merlin, agentic or GenAI despite Merlin, Agentic AI and ANA being in product scope. Per the 8 Dec note: 'strong meeting with proc & IT teams... real pain points and strong leaning to Zycus. CTO (John) recommended they speak to us.'
• Per the 14 Jan note: 'Value justification needed- session confirmed with Sherald and team- she wants to present to exco by 22nd Jan for approval.' Per 20 Jan: 'final workshop (B'Case/ROI on Fri 23rd F2F to get sign off with mgmt by 30th Jan- non competitive.' Per 6 Feb: 'shared commercials for Mgmt sign off.' Buyer has strong champion network (proc + IT + CTO endorsement) and is in value-justification/EXCO approval mode ‚Äî but conversation is anchored in ROI and management buy-in, not AI capability.
• Confidence is Medium. Gamuda has three AI products in scope and a strong multi-stakeholder champion network with CTO endorsement, but the captured dialogue across 8 entries is exclusively ROI/EXCO-approval/value-justification with no AI keyword from buyer side. Score 6 (boundary DOWN per rubric ‚Äî would flip to 7 only with an explicit buyer AI quote).

north_star_verdict.headline: Non-competitive deal won on merit but now cold: Sherald Yap presented the business case to EXCO on 23 Jan 2026, but on 13 Mar 2026 Gamuda emailed that it 'will not be further exploring Zycus and other procurement system', and there has been no two-way buyer reply in the ~115 days since, including no answer to the 12/13 Jun rep outreach.
north_star_verdict.math: Today 6 Jul 2026. Close 30 Nov 2026 = 147 days out. Moved Formal Evaluation -> Shortlisted 1 Jun 2026 (rep-side re-stage). Last incoming buyer email 13 Mar 2026 (~115 days ago) and it was a stand-down message; LastActivityDate 13 Jun 2026 is a rep-side event invite with no reply. Cold 60+ days with a buyer pause message forces Off Track regardless of stage. EXCO sign-off targeted 'by 30th Jan' has slipped ~5 months; realistic close is 2027 unless the pause is reversed.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (downgrade from Best Case: buyer stood down on 13 Mar 2026, no engaged economic buyer, EXCO approval stalled ~5 months, no two-way buyer touch in ~115 days; re-promote only on a dated re-engagement / EXCO approval signal).
evidence: Task 13 Mar 2026 (email received from Gamuda): 'At this point, GL will not be further exploring Zycus and other procurement system as we have identified several key i...' - an explicit stand-down from the buyer.

Next_Step__c: '1st Jun KIV and waiting on budget validation internally' and '12th Jun f2F to update status'; Task 13 Jun 2026 is a rep-side Singapore procurement-event invite to Yee Mei/Sherald with no logged reply.
Next_Step__c: '23rd Jan Fit Gap and Business case presented'; '20th Jan final workshop B'Case/ROI ... get sign off with mgmt by 30th Jan- non competitive' - approval target missed by ~5 months.

metrics.narrative: On the 19 Nov 2025 intro call Sherald Yap framed the value qualitatively: speed on low-risk 'fuel spend' construction items, eliminating the '2 or 3 pages kind of input in the BR', centralising a decentralised procurement function so non-procurement business users ('sales, marketing team') stop doing buying work, and fixing dual invoicing off the Alibaba portal. She anchored the cost bar herself, noting the incumbent was already a seven-figure spend ('iRequest is also 1,000,000 dollar') and that any change must justify 'another million dollars to buy a new one.' No hard ROI %, savings figure, or cycle-time target has been quantified in the read evidence, so the value case is still qualitative. That un-quantified value case is the most likely root cause of the EXCO stall and the 13 Mar stand-down, so producing a numbers-based savings model on tail/low-risk spend is the move.
sources: Next_Step__c (23 Jan business case; 6 Feb commercials)

economic_buyer.narrative: The budget decision sits with the relevant stakeholder / top management, and it is the live blocker: Next_Step__c shows the business case went to management for sign-off (23 Jan 2026, target 'by 30th Jan') and as of 1 Jun 2026 the deal is 'waiting on budget validation internally.' On the 19 Nov 2025 call Sherald referenced needing top-management alignment ('every dollar is back, it's an investment company') and predicted a CFO would demand 'a business case ... an ROI.' No individual economic buyer is engaged as a named contact role and none appears as a speaker on the read call. Loo Yee Mei (Senior General Manager, Decision Maker per contact role) is the most senior mapped contact and the nearest lever to budget authority, but she has not been confirmed as the budget owner. Reaching and mapping the actual EXCO budget owner, brokered through Sherald and Loo Yee Mei, is the single highest-leverage gap.
sources: OpportunityContactRole (Loo Yee Mei, Senior General Manager, Decision Maker)
Next_Step__c (1 Jun budget validation; 23 Jan mgmt sign-off)

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are reconstructable from the 19 Nov 2025 call and the 23 Jan fit-gap milestone: agentic AI to automate low-risk/tail (fuel) spend with 'minimum human interventions', catalog management to feed the agent ('categorize it into right basket'), a single-entry guided UX ('one entry point, puts in their chat whatever they need') with policy-compliance enforcement, and deep certified integration with SAP S/4HANA and the existing Alibaba network. Bharti (IT) added a forward criterion on the same call: the Merlin intake front end must work once Gamuda migrates from the relevant stakeholder to the relevant stakeholder/Google Chat in Jan 2026, which Akshay answered with a web-app workaround and a roadmap commitment. A fit-gap and business case were 'presented' 23 Jan 2026, but a formal weighted criteria/BRD document is not in the read evidence.
sources: Avoma intro call 19 Nov 2025 (Sherald Yap, Bharti, Akshay Dhanda)

decision_process.narrative: The process reads cleanly off the call/next-step sequence: 19 Nov 2025 overview -> 8 Dec 2025 F2F with procurement + IT ('strong leaning to Zycus', CTO 'John' recommended Zycus) -> 14-23 Jan 2026 value-justification and final ROI/business-case workshop with Sherald's team -> management sign-off targeted 'by 30th Jan' -> as of 1 Jun 2026 'KIV waiting on budget validation internally.' The gating step is an internal EXCO budget approval that Sherald, a Procurement Manager, cannot clear alone. That approval has stalled ~5 months past its target, and the 13 Mar 2026 buyer email ('will not be further exploring') indicates the internal process paused rather than advanced. The process is understood; the risk is that it has stopped inside the buyer's org.

paper_process.narrative: Commercials were 'shared for Mgmt sign off' on 6 Feb 2026, but no MSA/SOW, legal redline, procurement channel, or InfoSec review activity is recorded, and SAP S/4HANA + Alibaba integration scoping (which the buyer explicitly flagged as a prerequisite on 19 Nov) is not formalised. With budget approval still pending and the buyer stood down since 13 Mar, paper has not started. As a Malaysia/APAC deal a PO would gate invoicing if it reaches contracting, but that is far downstream. Naming a paper/contracting owner and laying out the post-approval integration-scoping and security path would create visibility once EXCO clears.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is specific and owned by Sherald Yap. On 19 Nov 2025 she described a decentralised procurement model where each department deputises a non-procurement user ('sales, marketing team') who 'don't really like to do procurement job', a slow, complex downstream P2P with '2, 3 pages kind of input in the BR' on low-risk spend, and dual invoicing off the Alibaba portal where suppliers self-invoice or an in-house user does it manually. She explicitly named people/change resistance as a second pain ('when comes to change, it's also talking about the people change'). This is confirmed, articulated operational pain with a named owner; the gap is that it has not been converted to a quantified EXCO case.

champion.narrative: Sherald Yap is the working champion: she drove the deal from the 19 Nov 2025 intro through the 8 Dec F2F and the Jan 2026 business-case/ROI workshops, and the trail records 'strong leaning to Zycus' and 'non competitive.' What has developed is genuine advocacy and a fit-gap/business-case presented to management; what is still missing is proven power reach into the EXCO/CFO who controls budget. The ~5-month approval stall, the 13 Mar 2026 stand-down email, and ~115 days of buyer silence signal she is either blocked internally or the deal has been deprioritised above her. She rates developing-trending-weak: re-arm her with a CFO-ready number and have her broker the budget-owner conversation, or the deal cannot advance on her authority alone.

competition.narrative: This is a non-competitive deal on vendors: Next_Step__c marks it 'non competitive' and no rival procurement vendor is bidding. The real competitor is the status quo / do-nothing: the incumbent Alibaba network (~5-6 years) plus SAP S/4HANA (~3 years) with an Ariba layer, and Sherald's own framing that Gamuda must justify spending 'another million dollars' over a functioning, already-seven-figure stack. Carl Kimball flagged the Ariba layer as the displacement target ('we'd love you to throw everything to do with Ariba away'), while supporting a hybrid co-existence path. The 13 Mar 2026 'will not be further exploring' email is the do-nothing threat materialising. The wedge is agentic AI on tail/low-risk spend and procurement centralisation the incumbent stack cannot deliver, quantified for EXCO.
sources: Avoma intro call 19 Nov 2025 (Sherald Yap, Carl Kimball, Akshay Dhanda)

deal_movement.summary: Deal advanced fast Nov 2025-Jan 2026 (intro -> F2F -> fit-gap/business case -> commercials) then stalled: buyer stood down on 13 Mar 2026 and has been silent ~115 days despite a 1 Jun re-stage to Shortlisted and a 13 Jun rep event invite.

Buyer emailed that Gamuda 'will not be further exploring' a new procurement system - stand-down
Rep re-staged Formal Evaluation -> Shortlisted; Next Step 'KIV waiting on budget validation internally'
Rep-side Singapore procurement-event invite sent to Yee Mei/Sherald; no reply logged

competitive_position.summary: Non-competitive on vendors; the single strongest CURRENT threat is status-quo inertia, now realised in the 13 Mar 2026 buyer stand-down. Gamuda must justify replacing a functional, already-seven-figure Alibaba + SAP S/4HANA/Ariba stack, and it has paused rather than fund the change. Win by quantifying agentic-AI savings on tail/low-risk spend and centralisation efficiency the incumbent cannot deliver, and arming Sherald with a CFO-ready ROI.

competitors.how_we_win: Quantify agentic-AI savings on tail/low-risk spend and centralisation efficiency the incumbent cannot deliver; arm Sherald with a CFO-ready ROI to reverse the do-nothing pause.

competitors.how_we_win: Position Merlin one-platform agentic source-to-pay and certified S/4HANA adapters as a superior replacement for the bolt-on Ariba layer, while supporting a hybrid co-existence path.

best_practice_check.summary: Cold, non-competitive deal blocked at the EXCO budget gate after a buyer stand-down; the win levers are reversing the pause, quantifying value, and reaching budget authority beyond a single mid-level champion.
flags: Re-open the relationship first: the 13 Mar 2026 'will not be further exploring' pause is unaddressed - diagnose the real objection ('several key issues') before any commercial step.
Convert Sherald's qualitative pain into a quantified, EXCO-ready ROI on tail/low-risk spend and centralisation - the un-numbered value case is the approval blocker.
Multi-thread beyond Sherald: reach Loo Yee Mei (Senior GM / Decision Maker) and the endorsing CTO to map the actual EXCO budget owner - the deal is single-threaded on a Procurement Manager.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Three AI products in scope (Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA) and genuine buyer curiosity on 19 Nov ('quite interested ... the agentic AI'), but every subsequent captured conversation is ROI/EXCO-approval with no AI keyword from the buyer - the agentic story sparked interest but has not been landed as the funded value driver.

The buyer opened the relationship on an agentic-AI hook ('quite interested ... back into that AI thingy') and wants autonomous tail-spend automation, but the dialogue since Dec 2025 is anchored on ROI and EXCO sign-off, not AI capability - curiosity that has not converted to a quantified AI value case.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Non-competitive deal won on merit but now cold at the EXCO budget gate: Sherald Yap presented the business case on 23 Jan 2026, but Gamuda emailed on 13 Mar 2026 that it 'will not be further exploring' a new system, and there has been no two-way buyer contact in the ~115 days since - the one thing between us and the deal is reversing a do-nothing pause, not beating a competitor.

win_position.text: Scored low for a Shortlisted deal because the buyer explicitly stood down on 13 Mar 2026 and has gone silent ~115 days, so the merit-based 'non competitive' lead cannot carry the number while the EXCO budget is unfunded.

We are the only vendor in play: Next_Step__c marks the deal 'non competitive' and the 8 Dec 2025 F2F recorded 'strong leaning to Zycus' with a CTO recommendation, so there is no rival to displace, only inertia to reverse.
The value case is qualitative only - Sherald quantified the cost bar ('another million dollars') but no ROI/savings number exists, which is the most likely reason EXCO paused.
Single-threaded on a mid-level Procurement Manager with no engaged economic buyer; the Senior GM decision-maker (Loo Yee Mei) has never been on a working call.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum has reversed: after fast progress Nov 2025-Feb 2026, the buyer's 13 Mar 2026 stand-down and 4-month silence mean the only recent motion is rep-side (a 13 Jun event invite with no reply).
The 1 Jun 2026 re-stage to Shortlisted was a rep action, not a buyer signal; no customer-named date or next meeting has been set since Jan 2026.

customer_commitment.text: Commitment has weakened: the buyer's most recent decisive statement (13 Mar 2026) is a withdrawal, not a commitment, reversing the earlier 'strong leaning to Zycus'.

Latent commitment exists - Gamuda invested in the business case through Jan 2026 and endorsed Zycus at CTO level, so the relationship is recoverable if the objection behind 'several key issues' is addressed.

deal_risk.text: Primary risk is do-nothing / status quo: the buyer must justify replacing a functional seven-figure Alibaba + SAP stack and chose to pause rather than fund it.
Secondary risk is political - no economic buyer engaged and the champion (Sherald) appears blocked or deprioritised above her level since 13 Mar 2026.

commentary.win_position: Win 28 is carried by business case strength +0.30 (weight 10). Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — Amount cut from $450k to $300k on 8 Dec 2025 as the deal moved Qualified -> Formal Evaluation, consistent with….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum is flat at 50: no forward or backward motion was observed.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 34 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found; Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 2 is anchored on the stage win (28), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 50, and dragged by risk 34. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
